action.reasoning: Observation: The CLIP ViT-B/16 model achieved 0.5628 balanced accuracy, but few-shot accuracy is still very low (0.2742) compared to many-shot (0.8185). Analysis: The dataset is highly imbalanced (long-tailed). To improve few-shot performance, we need to address this imbalance. We can use class-balanced loss re-weighting (inverse square root of class frequencies) and add data augmentation (RandAugment) to prevent overfitting on minority classes. Decision: Implement class weights in CrossEntropyLoss, add RandAugment, use CosineAnnealingLR, and train for 15 epochs. We will also save the best model based on validation accuracy.
